Transferring ownership to another indivi: Transferring ownership to another individual will revoke any future privileges to modify any visibility options.
We recommend turning this option OFF to: We recommend turning this option OFF to keep your todo's and notes private and secure. When this option is turned off, your Home todo's and notes will be accessible only from this device.
We recommend turning this option ON if y: We recommend turning this option ON if you notice any decrease in performance.
When enabled priority chips will be show: When enabled, the priority chips will be shown as a larger size and a full text chip.
When enabled the status chips will be sh: When enabled, the status chips will be shown as a larger size and a full text chip.
When enabled this option darkens the boa: When enabled, this option darkens the board column backgrounds for better visibility and contrast.
When enabled will show the best cover as: When enabled the task card will try showing the best possible cover aspect ratio at a smaller size. (Only in the board view)
When navigating through tasks using the: When navigating through tasks using the keyboard, a highlighted blue border will appear around the task. This visual cue indicates that you can perform a range of actions on the highlighted/selected tasks.
When turned on an overall percentage wil: When turned on an overall percentage will be shown for each Stack based on their tasks completness and progress.
When turned on it will hide the Add a ne: When turned on it will hide the `Add a new Stack` placeholder button from the Board view.
When using keyboard navigation to browse: When using keyboard navigation to browse tasks, the selected task's stack will be visually indicated by a highlighted blue border. This feature helps you easily identify the stack that will host any new task you add using keyboard shortcuts.
When working on a large number of stacks: When working on a large number of stacks on a single project, this is recommended. This option prevents stacks and all its contents from loading while they are not visible, improving speed and memory utilization.
